ai_agent.role: Construction scheduling coordinator managing 3-phase CPM pipeline across all active projects, tracking 66 activities per house from Clear Lot to Final Punch

core_knowledge.description: Master the full 66-activity CPM network covering all three construction phases. Understand dependencies, float, and critical path identification to prevent schedule slippage across the entire house construction pipeline.

Understand the four distinct process types used in the CPM: sequential (strict order), parallel (concurrent execution), milestone (gate checks), and contingency (weather/material delays). Each process type has different scheduling rules and buffer requirements.

Sequence: Clear Lot, Stake/Layout, Engineering Loads Calculation, Pad/Fill, Compaction Test, Form Foundation, Underground Plumbing (UP). Each step has county-specific permit and inspection requirements.
Sequence: Rough Plumbing, Drain Lines, Framing, Termite Treatment, Insulation, Drywall. Coordination with multiple trades working simultaneously requires careful sequencing to avoid conflicts.
Sequence: Paint, Tile, Vinyl/LVP, Cabinets, Trim/Millwork, Fixtures, Final Punch. Finish phase is most sensitive to change orders and material availability. Non-linear execution is common.
Maintain relationships with subcontractor crews, understand their capacity limits, scheduling preferences, and quality history. Know when and how to swap subs without disrupting the schedule.
Different counties (Marion, Citrus, Charlotte, etc.) have different inspection schedules, permit requirements, and workflow rules. Schedulers must know which rules apply to each project location.
Understand the pricing structure for each activity, how to read and update the price table, and how price changes affect budgets and subcontractor payments.
Track material availability for critical items (trusses, windows, cabinets, countertops). Late material changes (PP-08) are a major pain point requiring immediate schedule adjustment.

Understand that phases do not always execute in strict linear order. Phase 3 activities in particular may overlap, skip, or reorder based on material availability, trade crew scheduling, and site conditions.

daily_workflows.name: Phase 1 Scheduling Pipeline
steps: Check for new 'Survey Received' tags on projects
Verify engineering loads calculation is complete
Schedule Clear Lot with approved sub
Schedule Stake/Layout after lot clearing
Coordinate Pad/Fill delivery and equipment
Order Compaction Test after pad completion
Schedule Foundation Forming crew
Schedule Underground Plumbing (UP) after forms
Request Phase 1 inspection upon completion
critical_decisions: Whether compaction test results require re-work before proceeding
Which sub crew to assign based on current capacity
Whether weather delay requires full reschedule

daily_workflows.name: Phase 2 Scheduling Pipeline
steps: Verify Phase 1 inspection passed
Schedule Rough Plumbing and Drain Lines
Coordinate Framing crew and material delivery
Schedule Termite Treatment (must happen before insulation)
Schedule Insulation after all roughs pass inspection
Schedule Drywall delivery and hanging crew
Request Phase 2 inspections (multiple trades)
critical_decisions: Whether to overlap rough trades or sequence them

daily_workflows.name: Phase 3 Scheduling Pipeline
steps: Verify Phase 2 drywall completion and inspection
Schedule Paint crew (first finish trade)
Schedule Tile after paint (wet areas)
Schedule Vinyl/LVP flooring
Coordinate Cabinet delivery and installation
Schedule Trim/Millwork crew
Schedule Fixture installation (plumbing, electrical)
Schedule Final Punch walkthrough
Request final inspections
critical_decisions: Non-linear phase execution order based on material availability
Cabinet delivery date changes requiring full reschedule
Change order impact on finish timeline

daily_workflows.name: Draw Released Processing
steps: Receive Draw Released notification from Finance
Verify which phase the draw unlocks
Update project status to allow scheduling of next phase activities
Notify relevant phase scheduler to begin scheduling
Update BuilderTrend with phase unlock date
critical_decisions: Whether all prerequisites are met before unlocking phase

daily_workflows.name: Subcontractor Swap
steps: Identify underperforming or unavailable sub
Check alternative sub availability and pricing
Get approval for price differential if applicable
Update schedule with new sub assignment
Notify site supervisor of crew change
Send confirmation to new sub via WhatsApp/email
critical_decisions: Whether the price increase for replacement sub is acceptable
Whether delay from swap exceeds delay from waiting for original sub

handoffs.data_exchanged: Draw approval confirmation with phase identifier and funding amount
handoffs.impact: Phase cannot begin scheduling until draw is released; delays cascade to all downstream activities in that phase

handoffs.data_exchanged: Site survey data, lot dimensions, elevation, and engineering loads calculation results
handoffs.impact: Phase 1 cannot be initiated without survey data; missing 'Survey Received' tag (PP-07) causes houses to be skipped in scheduling queue

handoffs.data_exchanged: Updated floor plan with structural modifications, material changes, and dimensional adjustments
handoffs.impact: Phase 1 schedule must be updated; late changes (PP-08) require rework of sub assignments and material orders

handoffs.data_exchanged: Scheduled date, assigned subcontractor, scope of work, material requirements
handoffs.impact: Supervisors cannot plan daily routes without knowing which houses have scheduled activities

handoffs.data_exchanged: Pass/fail status, correction items if failed, re-inspection date
handoffs.impact: Failed inspections block scheduling of next phase activities; delays cascade through CPM

handoffs.data_exchanged: Completed activity list, sub payment amounts from price table, change order adjustments
handoffs.impact: Sub payments cannot be processed until scheduling confirms phase completion

pain_points.description: Triple data entry: schedulers must enter the same information into BuilderTrend, the internal platform, and WhatsApp group updates manually
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual copy-paste between systems; some schedulers maintain personal spreadsheets as intermediate step
pain_points.proposed_solution: Unified data entry with automatic sync across BuilderTrend and internal platform; WhatsApp integration via API for automated notifications

pain_points.description: Survey Received tag missing from projects causes houses to be invisible in scheduling queue, leading to delayed Phase 1 initiation
pain_points.current_workaround: Schedulers manually check with engineering weekly for unreceived surveys; some houses discovered only when supervisor asks why nothing is scheduled
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated Survey Received detection from engineering workflow; alert when project exists without survey tag beyond threshold days

pain_points.description: Late material changes (especially cabinets, windows, countertops) after scheduling is complete force full reschedule of affected activities and sub crews
pain_points.current_workaround: Schedulers manually identify all affected activities and contact each sub individually; often results in gaps where crews are reassigned elsewhere
pain_points.proposed_solution: Change order impact analysis tool that auto-identifies all affected activities in CPM and suggests reschedule options with sub availability check

pain_points.description: No visibility into subcontractor capacity across all active projects; schedulers may over-schedule a sub crew without knowing
pain_points.current_workaround: Schedulers call or WhatsApp subs to check availability before scheduling; relies on sub honesty about capacity
pain_points.proposed_solution: Sub capacity dashboard showing all scheduled work across projects with utilization percentage and conflict alerts

pain_points.description: Phase 3 non-linear execution makes template-based scheduling inaccurate; Daniela's mental heuristics are not codified
pain_points.current_workaround: Daniela personally reviews and adjusts Phase 3 schedules; other schedulers follow templates that may not reflect reality
pain_points.proposed_solution: Codify Phase 3 decision tree based on material availability, trade capacity, and site conditions into scheduling rules engine

tribal_knowledge.description: Daniela Santiago holds the mental model for when Phase 3 activities can be reordered. For example, if cabinet delivery is delayed 2+ weeks, she knows to pull tile and vinyl forward, but only if paint is fully cured. If trim carpenter is available but cabinets are not installed, she knows which trim items can be done pre-cabinet vs post-cabinet.

Each county has different scheduling rhythms. Marion County inspectors are available Monday-Thursday only. Citrus County requires portal submissions by 15:30 the day before. Charlotte County has no portal and requires site-only scheduling. These differences affect how far in advance activities must be scheduled.

Informal knowledge of which subs consistently show up on time, which ones need a day buffer, which ones do quality work but are slow, and which ones are fast but need more QC oversight. Not documented anywhere.

When rain is forecast, experienced schedulers know which activities can proceed (interior roughs, drywall if dried-in) and which must be rescheduled (foundation pour, pad compaction, exterior paint). Buffer days vary by activity type.

Certain floor plan types consistently take longer for engineering loads calculation. Schedulers who know these patterns can pre-schedule around expected delays rather than waiting for the calculation to complete.

topics: Critical Path Method theory and application
4 process types and their scheduling rules
Float calculation and critical path identification
Phase 3: Finish sequence and non-linear execution
Phase gate requirements and draw release triggers
Sub swap decision criteria and process
Weather and site condition adjustment factors
Variance tracking and schedule recovery techniques
Data quality checks and validation rules
Marion County inspection and permit rules
Citrus County portal deadlines and procedures

compliance_requirements.requirement: All scheduled activities must have valid permits before work begins
compliance_requirements.verification: Cross-reference scheduled activities with permit status in Inspecao department records before confirming sub schedule

compliance_requirements.requirement: Foundation work must pass compaction test before concrete pour is scheduled
compliance_requirements.verification: Compaction test results must be on file and passing before foundation forming crew is scheduled

compliance_requirements.requirement: Termite treatment must be completed before insulation is installed
compliance_requirements.verification: Termite treatment certificate must be uploaded before insulation activity can be scheduled

compliance_requirements.requirement: All rough inspections must pass before drywall can be scheduled
compliance_requirements.verification: Inspection results from Inspecao department must show all roughs (plumbing, electrical, mechanical) passed

compliance_requirements.requirement: Subcontractors must have current insurance and licensing on file
compliance_requirements.verification: Sub management module shows valid insurance and license expiration dates beyond scheduled work dates

key_metrics.target: >85% of activities completed within 1 day of scheduled date
key_metrics.measurement: Compare scheduled date vs actual completion date for each activity across all active projects

key_metrics.target: Phase 1: 15 days, Phase 2: 25 days, Phase 3: 30 days
key_metrics.measurement: Calendar days from first activity start to last activity completion per phase per house

key_metrics.target: <5% of scheduled activities result in no-show
key_metrics.measurement: Count of no-show incidents divided by total scheduled sub appointments per month

key_metrics.target: <15% of activities require rescheduling after initial scheduling
key_metrics.measurement: Count of rescheduled activities divided by total activities scheduled per month

key_metrics.target: Track total houses in each phase at any time
key_metrics.measurement: Weekly count of houses in Phase 1, Phase 2, Phase 3, and completed

key_metrics.target: <2 business days from draw release to first activity scheduled
key_metrics.measurement: Days between draw release notification and first scheduled activity in unlocked phase
